title: Post-Implementation Review (PIR) Completion Tracking
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" u_pir_required="true"
| eval pir_done=if(isnotnull(u_pir_completed) OR lower(u_pir_state)="closed",1,0)
| where pir_done=0
| eval age_days=round((now()-_time)/86400,0)
| where age_days>7
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, values(number) as chg by problem_id, short_description
| sort last_seen
description: PIRs capture lessons from major incidents and failed changes; tracking completion closes the feedback loop and satisfies audit expectations after Sev1 events.
value: PIRs capture lessons from major incidents and failed changes; tracking completion closes the feedback loop and satisfies audit expectations after Sev1 events.
implementation: Use change fields or related problem tasks for PIR workflow (`u_pir_required`, completion date). For Sev1-linked changes, join `problem` records and PIR tasks. Alert when PIR is overdue (e.g., 7 days post closure). Escalate to problem management owner.
visualization: Table (open PIRs), Single value (overdue PIR count), Bar chart (PIR completion SLA by team).
knownFalsePositives: Late completion when a major event is still open, legal review slows close-out, or a new runbook is not followed yet.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=snow:problem`, `sourcetype=snow:change_request`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Use change fields or related problem tasks for PIR workflow (`u_pir_required`, completion date). For Sev1-linked changes, join `problem` records and PIR tasks. Alert when PIR is overdue (e.g., 7 days post closure). Escalate to problem management owner.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" u_pir_required="true"
| eval pir_done=if(isnotnull(u_pir_completed) OR lower(u_pir_state)="closed",1,0)
| where pir_done=0
| eval age_days=round((now()-_time)/86400,0)
| where age_days>7
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, values(number) as chg by problem_id, short_description
| sort last_seen
```

Understanding this SPL

**Post-Implementation Review (PIR) Completion Tracking** — PIRs capture lessons from major incidents and failed changes; tracking completion closes the feedback loop and satisfies audit expectations after Sev1 events.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=snow:problem`, `sourcetype=snow:change_request`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:change_request. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:change_request". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **pir_done** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where pir_done=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>7` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by problem_id, short_description** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow change request list or form for the same records. Verify change numbers, states, and timing against our search. Use the same filters you expect operations to use in the list view.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (open PIRs), Single value (overdue PIR count), Bar chart (PIR completion SLA by team).
grandmaExplanation: We track whether we finish the after-change review, so the team learns and the same mistake is less likely to return.
